meta.title: Thumbs Up for Art and Music — Set 4
meta.subject: English Reading
meta.description: Fill-in and sentence practice for 'Thumbs Up for Art and Music.'

questions.question: Reading, writing, and _______ are important subjects in school.
questions.explanation: The text begins: 'You learn to do math problems.' Math (mathematics) involves working with numbers.

questions.question: What does 'learn' mean?
options: To gain new knowledge or skills
questions.explanation: 'Learn' means to gain new knowledge or skills. In school, 'you learn to read and write' — you gain new skills through study and practice.

questions.question: 'Problems' in 'math problems' means something is broken or wrong.
questions.explanation: In school, 'math problems' means math questions or exercises to solve — like '2 + 3 = ?' It doesn't mean something is wrong. 'Problem' has different meanings in different contexts.

questions.question: Art class is _______! It's the best reason to have art and music in school.
questions.explanation: 'Fun' means enjoyable. The last sentence of the text says 'They are fun!' — having a good time is the author's best reason for art and music.

questions.question: Which sentence uses 'think' correctly?
options: Art is important because it makes you think.
questions.explanation: 'Think' means to use your mind. 'Art is important because it makes you think' — it causes you to use your mind and brain. This is the correct use from the text.

questions.question: Arrange the words to form a sentence from the text:
questions.explanation: Correct sentence: 'You figure out how to make amazing things.' This is a reason from the text for why art is important — you learn to create impressive things.

questions.question: In music class, you have to _______ a lot — you keep all the notes in your head.
questions.explanation: 'Remember' means to keep knowledge in your mind. 'You have to remember a lot' means music requires you to hold notes, melodies, and timing in your memory.

questions.question: 'Creative' means being able to make something new and original using your own ideas.
questions.explanation: Yes! A 'creative' person thinks of new, original ideas and makes things that are unique. Art helps you be creative — you make things that come from your own imagination.

questions.question: Sort these words — are they POSITIVE (good) or can they be a CHALLENGE (hard)?
questions.explanation: 'Creative,' 'amazing,' and 'important' are clearly positive words. 'Concentrate,' 'problems,' and 'remember' describe things that require effort and hard work — they can be challenging!

questions.question: Art makes you _______. You use your brain and come up with good ideas.
questions.hint: To use your brain and mind
questions.explanation: 'Think' means to use your brain. The text says 'Art is important because it makes you think.' Art requires mental effort and creativity.

questions.question: The text says 'thumbs up' in the title. What is another expression with the same meaning?
questions.explanation: 'Thumbs up' means approval — 'I agree!' or 'Good job!' It's a positive gesture. The title 'Thumbs Up for Art and Music' means 'I approve of and support art and music!'

questions.question: Arrange the words to form a sentence about imagination:
questions.explanation: Correct sentence: 'You have to use your imagination.' This is what the text says about art — it requires you to use your imagination and think creatively.

questions.question: Which sentence uses 'concentrate' in the most natural way?
options: I concentrate the music very pretty.
She concentrates her piano — it is beautiful.
In music class, students must concentrate and focus carefully on each note.
questions.explanation: 'Concentrate' means to focus your attention. 'Students must concentrate and focus carefully' — this correctly uses 'concentrate' as an action that people do.

questions.question: Art helps you be _______. This means you can think of new and different ways to do things.
questions.hint: Able to make something new and original
questions.explanation: 'Creative' means thinking in new and original ways. Art helps students be creative — to make things that are uniquely theirs, using imagination and original thinking.

questions.question: The word 'important' appears many times in the text because the author wants to STRONGLY emphasize that art and music matter.
questions.explanation: Repeating 'important' is a persuasive technique. By saying 'Art is important' and 'Music is important' multiple times, the author hammers home the message that these subjects truly matter.
